name: Warm Goat Cheese and Potato Salad
recipeIngredient: 4 large Yukon gold or red potatoes
½ pound frisée / gourmet salad greens, torn
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut potatoes into rectangular blocks and dice the trimmings.
2. Pour oil to a depth of 3 inches in a Dutch oven; heat to 375°F. Fry diced potato in oil for 2 minutes or until golden. Drain and set aside. Fry potato blocks for 6 minutes or until golden. Drain and place in a shallow pan.
3. Bake at 375°F for 10 minutes or until tender.
4. Sprinkle diced potato and potato blocks with ½ teaspoon salt and ½ teaspoon pepper; cool.
5. Boil 1 cup balsamic vinegar in a heavy, nonstick skillet until reduced to ½ cup; set aside.
6. Cook bacon in the skillet until crisp; remove bacon, reserving ½ tablespoon drippings.
7. Add remaining ½ teaspoon salt, ½ teaspoon pepper, and ½ tablespoon balsamic vinegar to reserved drippings, stirring well.
8. Combine diced potato, bacon, frisée, and the next three ingredients in a large salad bowl; drizzle with balsamic dressing and toss gently.
9. Arrange salad evenly on individual salad plates; stand potato blocks upright in the center. Cut a slit in each potato block and insert 3 or 4 apple wedges into each slit.
10. Spoon Warm Goat Cheese Fondue onto plates; spoon over potato, if desired. Drizzle plate with reduced vinegar, Parsley Oil, and Chili Oil. Serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings
